scope.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0
scope.claim_policy: Use only tiered T0/T1 evidence for manuscript claims; no-IMU fallback rows are KITTI odometry evidence, not full LIO claims.

supporting_synthetic_stress_checks.name: RF-LIO/ID-LIO synthetic dynamic-object stress
supporting_synthetic_stress_checks.claim_limit: Mechanism stress only; public high-dynamic dataset validation is still required before manuscript-level dynamic-scene claims.

supporting_synthetic_stress_checks.name: M-GCLO synthetic non-flat ground stress
supporting_synthetic_stress_checks.claim_limit: Mechanism stress only; public non-flat dataset validation is still required before promoting M-GCLO to T0.

supporting_synthetic_stress_checks.name: Quadric-LO synthetic curved-object stress
supporting_synthetic_stress_checks.claim_limit: Mechanism stress only; public curved-object or non-urban dataset validation is still required before promoting Quadric-LO to T0.

remaining_before_full_manuscript_table: Quadric-LO public curved-object or non-urban dataset validation

methods.paper: Park, Jang, and Kim, I-LOAM: Intensity Enhanced LiDAR Odometry and Mapping, UR 2020
methods.claim: LiDAR reflectance improves LOAM odometry when used for correspondence search and residual weighting.
methods.mechanism: LiDAR reflectance in LOAM correspondence search and residual weighting.
methods.remaining_work: Add to manuscript table with the committed scan-to-scan intensity ablation locked.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0
protocol.reason_for_no_mapping: Mapping is disabled to isolate the paper-specific intensity correspondence and residual weighting in scan-to-scan odometry.

ablation.conclusion: The I-LOAM intensity path reduces scan-to-scan RPE drift by 18.2% on seq00 and 19.7% on seq07 under identical preprocessing, with active intensity weights around 0.76-0.77.

methods.paper: Tsin and Kanade, A Correlation-Based Approach to Robust Point Set Registration, ECCV 2004
methods.claim: Kernel correlation can register point sets without hard correspondences; coarse-to-fine sigma annealing broadens convergence.
methods.mechanism: Correspondence-free kernel correlation with optional coarse-to-fine sigma schedule.
methods.remaining_work: Add to manuscript table with fixed-sigma and annealed artifacts locked.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0

ablation.conclusion: Under KITTI full-sequence odometry with constant-velocity prediction, fixed fine sigma keeps KC-LO accuracy within 1% RPE of the annealed profile while improving throughput by about 1.9x on seq00 and 2.2x on seq07. The kernel-correlation mechanism is the main positive signal; sigma annealing appears to be a convergence-safety knob rather than necessary for this well-initialized protocol.

methods.paper: A Real-time Degeneracy Sensing and Compensation Method for Enhanced LiDAR SLAM
methods.claim: Condition-number degeneracy sensing is stable on KITTI; compensation is gated to real IMU packets.
methods.mechanism: Degeneracy-factor sensing with IMU/LiDAR compensation disabled on no-IMU KITTI so the constant-velocity fallback is diagnostic only.
methods.remaining_work: Validate the compensation path on a synchronized LiDAR-IMU benchmark before making full LIO claims.

protocol.type: no paired ablation in frozen bundle
protocol.claim_limit: Competitive KITTI fallback only; full LIO mechanism requires synchronized IMU validation.

ablation.conclusion: Condition-number degeneracy sensing is stable on KITTI; compensation is gated to real IMU packets.

methods.paper: D²-LIO: Enhanced Optimization for LiDAR-IMU Odometry Considering Directional Degeneracy
methods.claim: Directional degeneracy diagnostics are stable on KITTI; the directional prior is gated to real IMU packets.
methods.mechanism: Adaptive outlier gating plus Hessian eigen-direction diagnostics, with IMU prior regularization disabled on no-IMU KITTI.
methods.remaining_work: Validate the IMU-prior regularization path on a synchronized LiDAR-IMU benchmark before making full LIO claims.

protocol.type: no paired ablation in frozen bundle
protocol.claim_limit: Competitive KITTI fallback only; full LIO mechanism requires synchronized IMU validation.

ablation.conclusion: Directional degeneracy diagnostics are stable on KITTI; the directional prior is gated to real IMU packets.

methods.paper: Yang et al., M-GCLO: Multiple Ground Constrained LiDAR Odometry, ISPRS Annals X-1-2024
methods.claim: Multiple ground point-to-plane constraints improve vertical and attitude stability when fused with non-ground NDT correspondences.
methods.mechanism: Multiple ground point-to-plane constraints fused with non-ground NDT.
methods.remaining_work: Synthetic non-flat stress is committed; add public non-flat dataset validation before promoting to T0.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0

variants.ground_on: default multiple-ground point-to-plane constraints plus non-ground NDT
variants.ground_off: --m-gclo-no-ground after --m-gclo-dense-profile; classified ground correspondences are held out of the optimizer

ablation.conclusion: The ground factor is not needed to minimize local translational RPE on KITTI seq00/07, but disabling it increases rotational drift and more than doubles ATE on both sequences. Under this protocol, M-GCLO's multiple-ground constraint behaves as a stability/anchoring term rather than a pure translational-drift reducer.

methods.paper: Xia et al., Quadric Representations for LiDAR Odometry, Mapping and Localization, IEEE RA-L 2023 / arXiv:2304.14190
methods.claim: Point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals model curved local surfaces, with a point-to-plane fallback when a stable quadric cannot be fit.
methods.mechanism: Point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals with rare point-to-plane fallback.
methods.remaining_work: Synthetic curved-object stress is committed; add public curved-object or non-urban dataset validation before promoting to T0.

protocol.seed: first-pose anchor, --no-gt-seed velocity prediction after frame 0

variants.plane_fallback_on: default point-to-quadric residuals plus point-to-plane fallback for non-quadric neighborhoods
variants.plane_fallback_off: --quadric-lo-no-plane-fallback after --quadric-lo-dense-profile; non-quadric neighborhoods are held out of the optimizer

ablation.conclusion: Plane fallback is rare on KITTI seq00/07 (about 0.5-0.6% of used correspondences). Disabling it keeps Quadric-LO accuracy within about 1.5% translational RPE while improving throughput by 1.6-1.8x, so the committed KITTI evidence is dominated by the point-to-quadric path rather than the fallback.

methods.paper: Li et al., A Consistency-Improved LiDAR(-Inertial) Bundle Adjustment, arXiv:2602.06380
methods.claim: Stereographic plane parameterization is stable on KITTI; the committed no-BA profile optimizes local RPE over sliding-window BA on this IMU-free protocol.
methods.mechanism: Stereographic plane-normal front-end with optional sliding-window plane BA and FEJ gauge fix.
methods.remaining_work: Commit paired BA on/off artifacts before making BA trade-off claims in the frozen bundle.

protocol.type: no paired ablation in frozen bundle
protocol.claim_limit: Competitive KITTI fallback only; full LIO mechanism requires synchronized IMU validation.

ablation.conclusion: Stereographic plane parameterization is stable on KITTI; the committed no-BA profile optimizes local RPE over sliding-window BA on this IMU-free protocol.

methods.paper: Chetverikov et al., The Trimmed Iterative Closest Point Algorithm, ICPR 2002 / Image Vis. Comput. 2005
methods.claim: Least-trimmed-squares point-to-plane odometry is competitive with KISS-ICP on KITTI; FRMSD overlap sticks to the min_overlap floor on clean driving data.
methods.mechanism: Rank-based trimmed point-to-plane ICP with FRMSD automatic overlap estimation.
methods.remaining_work: Commit fixed-overlap vs auto-overlap ablation JSON before promoting trimming/overlap claims to T0.

protocol.type: no paired ablation in frozen bundle
protocol.claim_limit: Competitive KITTI fallback only; full LIO mechanism requires synchronized IMU validation.

ablation.conclusion: Least-trimmed-squares point-to-plane odometry is competitive with KISS-ICP on KITTI; FRMSD overlap sticks to the min_overlap floor on clean driving data.
